name: Solace (Inbound)
description: Solace inbound endpoint acts as a message consumer for Solace PubSub+ broker. It receives messages from configured topic/queue and inject them into the mediation flow.

params.name: name
params.description: A unique name for this Solace Inbound Endpoint configuration.

params.name: sequence
params.description: The name of the **mediation sequence** that will process the consumed Solace messages.

params.name: onError
params.description: The name of the **error handling sequence** to execute if an error occurs during message consumption or processing.

params.name: inbound.behavior
params.description: Defines the message consumption mode (e.g., event-based for immediate processing).

params.name: sequential
params.description: If enabled, messages will be processed one after the other within the injection sequence, preserving message order. Automatically enabled when autoAck is disabled, since client acknowledgement settles each message based on the mediation outcome.

params.name: coordination
params.description: In a clustered environment, set this to true to ensure that only a **single worker node** is consuming messages from the queue at any given time.

params.name: solace.host
params.description: Solace broker host (e.g., tcp://host:port, or tcps://host:port for TLS).

params.name: solace.vpnName
params.description: Solace Message VPN name.

params.name: solace.username
params.description: Solace username (for BASIC authentication).

params.name: solace.password
params.description: Solace password (for BASIC authentication).

params.name: solace.clientName
params.description: Optional unique client name for this connection. The broker generates one if omitted.

params.name: apiProvidedUsername
params.description: Username to send in the SMF login frame when authenticationScheme is CLIENT_CERTIFICATE. Ignored by the broker when it derives identity from the certificate common name.

params.name: solace.authenticationScheme
params.description: Authentication scheme: **BASIC**, **CLIENT_CERTIFICATE**, or **OAUTH2**.

params.name: solace.oauth2AccessToken
params.description: OAuth 2.0 access token (used when authenticationScheme is OAUTH2).

params.name: solace.oauth2IssuerIdentifier
params.description: Optional OAuth 2.0 issuer identifier.

params.name: solace.oidcIdToken
params.description: OIDC ID token (alternative to the OAuth 2.0 access token).

params.name: solace.destinationType
params.description: Destination type: **QUEUE** or **TOPIC**.

params.name: solace.destinationName
params.description: Solace queue or topic name. For a DIRECT topic subscription, a comma-separated list of topics is accepted.

params.name: solace.subscriptionType
params.description: Subscription type for topics: **DIRECT** or **DURABLE**.

params.name: solace.dteName
params.description: Durable Topic Endpoint name. Required when subscriptionType is DURABLE.

params.name: solace.selector
params.description: SQL-92 selector expression applied server-side on queue/DTE flows to filter messages.

params.name: solace.autoAck
params.description: If enabled, messages are acknowledged automatically. Disable for client acknowledgement, where the message is settled based on the mediation outcome (this forces sequential processing).

params.name: solace.subAckWindowSize
params.description: Transport acknowledgement window size for guaranteed messaging.

params.name: solace.failureOutcome
params.description: Action on injection failure: **NONE** (no ack; broker redelivery limit applies), **FAILED** (settle as FAILED → redelivered), or **REJECTED** (settle as REJECTED → routed to DMQ).

params.name: solace.contentType
params.description: Content-Type for inbound messages. Leave empty to auto-detect from the message's HTTP Content-Type header or payload. When set, it overrides that detection.

params.name: solace.binaryPayloadAsBase64
params.description: If enabled, binary (BytesMessage) payloads are Base64-encoded rather than UTF-8 decoded.

params.name: solace.connectionTimeout
params.description: Connection establishment timeout in milliseconds.

params.name: solace.connectRetries
params.description: Number of times to retry connecting before failing.

params.name: solace.connectRetriesPerHost
params.description: Number of connection attempts per host before moving to the next host.

params.name: solace.reconnectRetries
params.description: Number of times to retry reconnecting after the connection is lost.

params.name: solace.reconnectRetryWait
params.description: Wait time (in milliseconds) between reconnect attempts.

params.name: solace.sslTrustStorePath
params.description: File path to the truststore for TLS connections. If omitted, the JVM default truststore is used.

params.name: solace.sslTrustStorePassword
params.description: Password for the truststore.

params.name: solace.sslTrustStoreFormat
params.description: Truststore format (e.g., JKS).

params.name: solace.sslKeyStorePath
params.description: File path to the keystore for mutual TLS (client certificate authentication).

params.name: solace.sslKeyStorePassword
params.description: Password for the keystore.

params.name: solace.sslKeyStoreFormat
params.description: Keystore format (e.g., JKS).

params.name: solace.sslKeyPassword
params.description: Password for the private key within the keystore.

params.name: solace.sslValidateCertificate
params.description: If enabled, the server certificate is validated on TLS connections. **Disabling is not recommended for production.**

params.name: solace.sslValidateCertificateDate
params.description: If enabled, the server certificate's validity dates are checked on TLS connections.

params.name: solace.generateReceiveTimestamps
params.description: If enabled, JCSMP stamps a receive timestamp on incoming messages.

params.name: solace.provisionDestination
params.description: If enabled, the inbound endpoint provisions the queue or Durable Topic Endpoint if it does not already exist.

params.name: solace.queueAccessType
params.description: Access type for a provisioned queue: **EXCLUSIVE** or **NON_EXCLUSIVE**.

params.name: solace.queuePermission
params.description: Permission for a provisioned queue: **CONSUME**, **DELETE**, **MODIFY_TOPIC**, or **READ_ONLY**.

params.name: solace.queueQuotaMB
params.description: Quota (in MB) for a provisioned queue. 0 leaves the broker default.

params.name: solace.queueMaxMsgSize
params.description: Maximum message size (in bytes) for a provisioned queue. 0 leaves the broker default.

params.name: solace.queueRespectTTL
params.description: If enabled, a provisioned queue respects the message time-to-live (TTL).

params.name: solace.queueMaxMsgRedelivery
params.description: Maximum redelivery attempts for a provisioned queue before dead-lettering. 0 leaves the broker default.
